rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF BEATRICE FORSTEIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the mandatory Judicial Council form Summary of Account was not attached to the accounting, and $867.85 was paid to the attorney on 5/24/22 without the amount having been ordered or itemized to show what the costs entailed. In interim accounts, only the statements verifying the ending balances need to be filed, not each statement for the accounting period. [Probate Code Sect. 2620(c)(2).] No facility statements were filed, as alleged in paragraph 3 of the report

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FREDERICK HOSKING
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/12/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of notice of the amendment has been filed. [CRC 7.53(a).] The only correct form of notice for a petition for final distribution or an amendment thereto is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to it. (2) Notice must include the procedures for making a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Administrator requests to withhold a $35,000 reserve, but this is still missing from the prayer. (4) The prayer in the amendment still refers to an account, does not include the reserve, and requests distribution "as set forth in the petition." A second amendment to the petition must be filed to address these issues. Notice must be given after the second amendment is e-filed, unless the other party waives notice. Deadline is 3/1/24 for providing the assigned examiner a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain TWO PREPUNCHED NORMAL-SIZED HOLES, centered 2-1/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a FOOTER IN THE BOTTOM MARGIN OF EACH PAGE, PLACED BELOW THE PAGE NUMBER AND DIVIDED FROM THE REST OF THE DOCUMENT PAGE BY A PRINTED LINE. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and REPRESENTATIVE CAPACITY, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALBERT A. STEPICEVICH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/19/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have the instructions for remote appearance procedures attached to it. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (2) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (3) Did the petitioner or administrator ever address whether or not Decedent's predeceased cousin Robert Zurich was survived by issue? Neither the amended petition filed on 10/4/22 nor the family tree attached to it addressed this and there was no supplement to the amended petition. (4) Felicia Zurich's creditor's claim included $700 paid to Loren Lopin for "filing fees, Letter." (5) There was also another creditor's claim. (6) No allowance or rejection was filed for either claim, as required by Probate Code Section 9250(b). (7) Whey were there so many losses on CDs? (For some reason, the accounting pages were entirely backwards, like a Japanese novel, so it was challenging to review.) (8) If the FMV differs from (is lower than) the $156,874.35 CV shown on Schedule E, how does the administrator propose to make the exact distributions? (9) Paragraph 32 shows Ferdo Frantin twice but not Marinko Frantin. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/8/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: The attorney represented the initial petitioner, Felicia Zurich, and now the administrator, who was somehow able, during a transitional time at this Court, to file an amended Petition for Letters of Administration, when he had never filed his own petition. When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 2-1/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIE ANDREWS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues have not been cleared: (1) Notice of Petition to Administer Estate [DE-121] was mailed; however, it failed to affirm #5 to mirror that the petition seeks IAEA authority. CRC 7.50. (2) Notice of Direction for Remote Appearances and Access to be served with the notice. LRSF 14.1.C.6). (3) Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative [DE-147] to be filed. (4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number [JC Form DE-147S] to be filed. See the Local Rule of SF 14.18.E. (5) Petition #8 to identify each person's relationship to the decedent as the form instructs. (6) Petition #8 also needs to identify the trustee of the sole devisee trust. Depending on who the trustee is, trust beneficiaries need to be listed and noticed, if applicable. PrC sec.1208. (7) Petition #3.d to clarify the estimated value of the property, whether all $750K is for personal property only or real property only. (8) Petition #5.a. (3)-(4) to be clarified re: whether the decedent was or was not survived by a registered domestic partner. (9) Petitioner used the Judicial Council Form DE-111 by marking "Supplement" in the caption, which appears inappropriate. A verified supplement to petition, an amended petition, or an amendment to petition is needed. CRC 7.3 and 7.53. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHIN HSIN LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/6/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: For some reason, a new Spousal Property Petition (SPP) was filed on 12/18/23. Now, there are two petitions before the Court. The new SPP is not properly verified. No proof of service of the new SPP has been filed. The proof service filed on 12/18/23 is for the Amended SPP. Instructions for remote appearance were not attached to that proof of service, and item 5 was not checked, indicating that a copy of the petition and other documents were served with the Notice of Hearing. Petitioner might wish to withdraw the new SPP and supplement his Amended SPP. If the decedent's parents' consents and waivers of notice were filed, the Court could waive procedural defects and either SPP could be pre-granted without an appearance. Deadline for addressing these issues is 2/26/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Assets Of A Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/05/2024 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 02/08/2024). The following issues (see tentative ruling for 01/02/2024 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) The proposed order must include the legal description of the property. 2) Copies of the DE-115 form and petition must be served on Michelle Rathbun at least 30 days before the hearing. See Probate Code sections 851(b)(3) & 17203(a)(2). The proof of service on file shows notice was given on 12/16/2023, less than 30 days before the 01/02/2024 hearing. New 30-day notice of the continued hearing must be given, absent a signed waiver. 3) Beverly Bratek must be served in the manner required by sections 851(b)(3) & 17203(b) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. applicable to service of a civil summons. The mere mailing is not an authorized manner of service. See, e.g., CCP sections 415.30 and 415.40. 4) A copy of the supplement must be served. A proof of service must be filed. See CRC 7.53(b). 5) A declaration required by CRC 7.52(a) must be filed to describe efforts to identify and locate heirs of the predeceased spouse under section 6402.5(a)(1)-(3). 6) The legal description of the property set forth in the preliminary title report is different from the legal description contained in the petition. 7) The preliminary title report indicates that the petitioner as trustee already holds record title to one-half of the property. If so, the scopie of relief available under section 850(a)(3)(B) appears to be limited to the remaining one-half of the property titled in the predeceased spouse's name as his community property interest.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PATRICIA A. LURATI 2020 (IRREVOCABLE) TRUST, DATED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Successor Trustee And Trust Assets Under California Probate.
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/20/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no proposed order has been submitted or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must include the instructions for remote appearance procedures attached to it. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) This does not appear to be the correct venue if the successor trustee resides in Folsom. Please provide the proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/11/24 if the petition is not withdrawn. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The estate of Robert D. Lurati was case PES-15-298536, not -298538.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARGIE VICTOR LIVING TRUST ESTABLISHED MARCH 29, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: Objections To Fiduciary'S Account And Request For Surcharge Of Fiduciary And Other Remedies
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. The petition has not yet been reviewed. The Court to first determine whether venue is properly in San Francisco. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE CASTILYN FAMILY REVOCABLE TRUST, DATED AUGUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/19/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) Please file a copy of the current deed and the bank statements immediately preceding the date of the deceased trustor's death to show how title to the real property and the accounts was vested. (4) The petition does not appear to set forth the full legal description of the real property. (5) How old is the minor? Who represents her interests? Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/8/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAUL H. NATHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
